name: Basic Southern Tomato Sauce
recipeInstructions:
This is a typical southern Italian sauce. If you like it spicier, add
peperoncino (red pepper flakes).
1. Coarsely chop the tomatoes on a plate, saving the liquid they release.
2. Halve the garlic clove.
3. Heat the olive oil in a broad sauté pan and simmer the garlic in it until lightly golden. Remove the garlic pieces from the pan.
4. Raise the heat to medium-high and add the chopped tomatoes and their juices from the plate. Simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes, until the sauce is lightly thickened.
5. Add the chopped parsley and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Stir everything together once.
6. Remove the sauce from the heat. Makes 2 1/2 Cups
